title: crotalaria gajureliana
intro: Crotalaria gajureliana, also known as Gajurela or Gajurela bush, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to the Himalayas and is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its ability to treat digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of Crotalaria gajureliana can be found in the form of decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are often used to treat various ailments in traditional medicine.
